name: Microorganisms: Friend and Foe
subject: biology
summary: Tiny organisms you can't see — some make bread rise, some make you sick.

crushed.title: Micro = too small to see
crushed.text: Need a microscope. Four main types: bacteria, viruses, fungi, protozoa.

crushed.title: Friends: food & medicine
crushed.text: Yeast ferments dough. Bacteria make curd, antibiotics, and fix nitrogen in soil.

crushed.title: Foes: disease
crushed.text: Viruses cause flu & COVID. Bacteria cause cholera & TB. Protozoa cause malaria.

crushed.title: Vaccines train immunity
crushed.text: Weakened germs teach your body to recognize and fight the real thing.

crushed.title: Hygiene kills germs
crushed.text: Boiling, pasteurization, and disinfectants stop microbial growth.

items.label: Bacteria
items.detail: Single-celled prokaryotes. Some helpful (nitrogen fixers), some harmful (pathogens)

items.label: Viruses
items.detail: Not truly alive — need a host cell to reproduce. Cause many diseases

items.label: Fungi
items.detail: Yeast, moulds, mushrooms. Decomposers + bread/alcohol makers

flows.title: How Milk Becomes Curd
flows.description: Lactobacillus bacteria turn milk sugar into lactic acid — curdling the milk.

steps.label: Warm milk
steps.description: A spoon of curd contains millions of Lactobacillus bacteria.

steps.label: Bacteria
steps.description: Warm temperature (30–40°C) is ideal for bacterial growth.

steps.label: Milk protein
steps.description: Acid denatures casein — milk solids clump together.

steps.label: Result
steps.description: Thick, tangy curd ready in 6–8 hours.

keyTerms.definition: Organism visible only under a microscope

keyTerms.analogy: Unwanted guest that makes you sick

keyTerms.definition: Anaerobic breakdown of sugars by microbes
keyTerms.analogy: Bacteria eating sugar and burping acid

demos.title: Curd Formation by Lactobacillus
demos.description: Warm milk + bacteria → lactic acid → milk proteins clump into curd.

steps.label: Add starter
steps.description: A spoon of curd (Lactobacillus) is mixed into warm milk.

steps.label: Bacteria multiply
steps.description: Bacteria ferment lactose sugar into lactic acid.

steps.label: Curd sets
steps.description: Acid lowers pH — milk proteins coagulate into soft curd.

chapterSummary.overview: Microorganisms are tiny living things invisible to the naked eye — bacteria, fungi, protozoa, algae, and viruses. This chapter covers their types, useful roles (food, medicine, cleaning), harmful effects (diseases), and how to prevent them through hygiene and preservation.
keyPoints: Microorganisms are classified as bacteria, fungi, protozoa, algae, and viruses.
Friendly microbes: Lactobacillus makes curd; yeast ferments bread and alcohol; Rhizobium fixes nitrogen.
Harmful microbes cause diseases like cholera, tuberculosis, malaria, and food poisoning.
Preservation methods — pasteurisation, refrigeration, salting, and chemical preservatives — slow microbial growth.
Vaccines train the immune system; antibiotics kill bacteria but not viruses.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Give examples of useful vs harmful microorganisms. Explain how curd forms and how Louis Pasteur discovered pasteurisation.
